data.title: Influencers Face Challenges in Determining Tax Write-Offs for Social Media Content
data.description: Social media influencers are encountering difficulties in distinguishing what qualifies as a tax write-off when their content involves makeup, pets, and meals. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the complexities influencers face in navigating tax regulations. As influencers often blend personal and professional expenses, determining which costs are deductible can be challenging. The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) requires that expenses be ordinary and necessary for business to qualify as deductions. However, the line between personal and business expenses can blur, especially when influencers incorporate everyday activities into their content. This ambiguity has led to increased scrutiny from tax authorities, prompting influencers to seek professional advice to ensure compliance. As the influencer industry continues to grow, understanding tax obligations remains crucial for content creators.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Shenzhen's Water Bay Sees Surge in Gold Purchases During Spring Festival
data.description: Shenzhen's Water Bay area has experienced a significant increase in gold purchases during the Spring Festival, with many stores remaining open throughout the holiday period. According to Jin10, numerous tourists from other provinces have traveled specifically to buy gold, making Water Bay a must-visit destination in Shenzhen. A manager at a Water Bay gold store reported a more than 30% year-on-year increase in customer traffic. Experts attribute this rise in gold consumption to the combination of holiday shopping demand and the festive season, resulting in both increased volume and price.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Israel Approves New Airport Construction Near Gaza
data.description: Israel's cabinet has given the green light for the development of a new international airport on the periphery of Gaza. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic significance of this decision amid ongoing regional tensions. The airport aims to enhance connectivity and bolster economic growth in the area, providing a new gateway for international travel and trade. This move comes as part of broader infrastructure initiatives by the Israeli government to improve transportation networks and stimulate economic activity in the region. The decision reflects Israel's commitment to advancing its infrastructure despite geopolitical challenges.

data.title: BlackRock Executive Warns of Bitcoin Volatility Due to Excessive Leverage
data.description: BlackRock's head of digital assets has raised concerns about the impact of excessive leverage on crypto derivatives platforms, which is contributing to heightened volatility in Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, this speculation poses a risk to Bitcoin's reputation as a stable hedge asset. The warning underscores the potential destabilizing effects of leveraged trading on the market perception of Bitcoin.

data.title: Mike Johnson Faces Challenges as Speaker of the House
data.description: Speaker of the House Mike Johnson holds a prestigious position but faces significant challenges in his role. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that Johnson's difficulties are largely self-inflicted. As he navigates the complexities of his position, Johnson must address various issues that come with the territory of being Speaker. His leadership will be tested as he works to manage the responsibilities and expectations associated with his role.

data.title: Russia's Finance Ministry Reports $648 Million Daily Crypto Transactions Amid Regulatory Changes
data.description: Russia's finance ministry has disclosed that daily cryptocurrency transactions in the country amount to approximately $648 million, highlighting significant activity in the unregulated market. According to NS3.AI, lawmakers are anticipated to pass new regulations in the upcoming spring session aimed at licensing exchanges and regulating market participants. Major financial entities, such as the Moscow Exchange and various banks, are gearing up to enter the cryptocurrency market once the legal framework is in place.

data.title: New Orleans Faces Budget Crisis Amid Mardi Gras Celebrations
data.description: New Orleans is currently facing a significant budget crisis as Mardi Gras festivities draw crowds to the city. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the financial strain is primarily due to the depletion of pandemic aid, rising expenses, and financial decisions that have brought the city near junk status. The situation poses challenges for the local government as it navigates these economic difficulties while hosting one of its largest annual events.

data.title: Justice Department Reforms Needed Post-Trump Administration
data.description: Restoring the integrity of the Department of Justice will necessitate structural reforms following U.S. President Donald Trump's tenure. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the need for changes after instances of politicized prosecutions, pardons for loyalists, and other forms of political manipulation. These actions have raised concerns about the impartiality and independence of the Justice Department, prompting calls for reforms to ensure its proper functioning in the future.

data.title: Ethereum Stabilizes Near $2,060 as Analysts Outline 2026 Recovery Scenarios
data.description:
Ethereum is trading around $2,060, consolidating after rebounding from recent lows near $1,850–$1,880. The 1-hour structure shows a recovery attempt into resistance rather than a confirmed breakout, with price compressing beneath the $2,080–$2,120 region.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/ethereum-stabilizes-near-2060-as-analysts-outline-2026-recovery-scenarios/

data.title: Russia Accelerates Digital Ruble Launch to Reduce US Dollar Influence in BRICS Trade
data.description: Russia is fast-tracking the introduction of its digital ruble to diminish the US dollar's dominance in BRICS trade. According to NS3.AI, the digital ruble is designed mainly for international trade within the BRICS bloc, with China likely to be the first to adopt it. Key Russian financial authorities and BRICS central banks are collaborating on this initiative, with further discussions anticipated at the forthcoming BRICS summit in India.

data.title: NASDAQ Removes Limits on Bitcoin ETFs
data.description: Crypto Rover posted on X that NASDAQ has announced the removal of all limits on Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs). This decision marks a significant development in the cryptocurrency market, potentially increasing accessibility and investment opportunities for Bitcoin ETFs. The move is expected to attract more investors and could lead to increased trading volumes. The removal of limits may also influence other financial institutions to reconsider their stance on cryptocurrency investments. This change comes amid growing interest in digital assets and their integration into mainstream financial systems.

data.title: Johannes Hosflot Klaebo Sets Winter Olympics Gold Medal Record
data.description: Johannes Hosflot Klaebo made history on Sunday by breaking the all-time Winter Olympics gold medal record, securing his ninth career gold. New York Times posted on X that Klaebo's achievement came as Norway triumphed in the men's cross-country skiing team relay. This victory solidifies Klaebo's status as one of the most successful athletes in Winter Olympics history, showcasing his exceptional talent and dedication to the sport. Norway's performance in the relay further highlights the country's dominance in winter sports, continuing a tradition of excellence on the global stage.

data.title: Canada to Boost Military Spending with Domestic Firms
data.description: Canada plans to allocate a larger portion of its expanding military budget to domestic companies, aiming to stimulate over C$500 billion ($369 billion) in investments over the next ten years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's strategy to bolster national defense capabilities while supporting local industries. This initiative is part of a broader effort to enhance Canada's military infrastructure and technological advancements. The move is expected to create significant economic opportunities within the country, fostering growth and innovation in the defense sector.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 2,000 USDT with a 3.60% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 15, 2026, 13:51 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,000 USDT and is now trading at 1,999.26001 USDT, with a narrowed 3.60% decrease in 24 hours.
